articles.title: Urban Air Trampoline Park Agrees to $300K Settlement After Go-Kart Death of 6-Year-Old
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park in Port St. Lucie, Florida has agreed to pay $300,000 following an investigation into a go-kart crash that killed a six-year-old child. The settlement carries major implications for FEC operators running go-kart attractions, highlighting the critical importance of ride safety protocols, age and height restrictions, and liability management. FEC operators should review their own safety procedures and insurance coverage in light of this case.

articles.title: Florida Settles with Family of 6-Year-Old Killed in FEC Go-Kart Crash
articles.summary: Florida authorities have reached a $300,000 settlement with the family of a child killed in a go-kart accident at a family entertainment center. The case underscores the regulatory and legal risks associated with go-kart and high-speed attractions in FEC environments. Operators should treat this as a prompt to audit safety standards, staff training, and their exposure to liability claims.

articles.title: Raw Thrills Launches Stranger Things Arcade Redemption Game for Up to 4 Players
articles.summary: Raw Thrills has globally launched Stranger Things Arcade, a family-friendly multiplayer redemption game themed around the hit Netflix series, supporting up to four players across iconic locations like Hawkins Lab and Starcourt Mall. The title leverages one of the most-watched streaming franchises in history to drive arcade floor traffic and repeat play. FEC operators looking to refresh their redemption mix with a recognizable IP should take note of this new cabinet.

articles.title: Intercard Hires Bowling Industry Veteran JR Huyck as North American Sales Manager
articles.summary: Intercard, a leading cashless payment technology provider for FECs and amusement venues, has appointed bowling industry veteran JR Huyck as its North American Sales Manager. Huyck brings deep roots in the bowling sector, a key FEC vertical, signaling Intercard's continued push to expand its cashless solutions across the industry. This hire may accelerate adoption of Intercard's technology among bowling-centric FEC operators.

articles.title: Intercard Showcases New Cashless Technology at Asia Amusement & Attractions Expo
articles.summary: Intercard is presenting its latest cashless payment innovations, including the new Impulse system, at the Asia Amusement & Attractions Expo in Guangzhou, China, running May 10-12. The expo is a key sourcing and trend-spotting event for amusement operators globally, and Intercard's presence highlights the growing international demand for card-based and cashless venue management. FEC operators evaluating cashless upgrades should watch for product announcements coming out of the show.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named One of USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has been recognized by USA Today as one of the most trusted brands by parents, a significant reputational milestone for the FEC chain as it continues its post-bankruptcy brand rebuild. Trust and safety perception are key drivers of FEC visitation among family demographics, making this recognition competitively meaningful. Independent FEC operators can use this benchmark to evaluate their own community reputation and family-focused brand positioning.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Announced for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, adding to the growing pipeline of FEC developments in suburban markets across the US. Mt. Juliet is one of the fastest-growing communities in the Nashville metro area, making it an attractive target for leisure and entertainment investment. This opening reflects continued operator confidence in suburban FEC demand.

articles.title: Kidtopia Indoor Playground Opens in Dublin with Obstacle Courses and Climbing
articles.summary: Kidtopia, a new indoor playground concept, is coming to Dublin featuring obstacle courses, indoor climbing, and active play attractions aimed at younger children. The concept reflects a broader trend of indoor active-play venues targeting the sub-10 age demographic, a segment that remains strong in FEC markets. Operators in the indoor playground and trampoline park space should monitor how Kidtopia's format competes with and complements traditional FEC offerings.

articles.title: Black Bunny Blends Gaming, Adventure and Family Entertainment in Dubai
articles.summary: Black Bunny, a new entertainment venue in Dubai, combines gaming, adventure activities, and family-friendly programming under one roof, reflecting the growing FEC hybrid model gaining traction in the Middle East. The venue's multi-concept approach mirrors strategies being deployed by successful FEC operators globally to drive longer dwell times and broader demographic appeal. International FEC developers and suppliers should take note of the Middle East as an increasingly active growth market.

articles.title: WhiteWater Opens 60+ Water Attraction Projects Worldwide in Record Year
articles.summary: WhiteWater, the world's largest water attraction manufacturer, is highlighting more than 60 project openings globally in 2026, spanning waterparks, water rides, surf lagoons, and FlowRider installations. The volume of openings signals robust capital investment in water-based entertainment, a sector that overlaps significantly with FEC and resort entertainment. FEC operators considering aquatic additions or expansions should be aware of the broad range of WhiteWater products now proven in market.

articles.title: WhiteWater Highlights Record-Breaking Water Attraction Projects Across the Globe
articles.summary: WhiteWater has spotlighted standout projects from its 60-plus 2026 openings, including the record-breaking Aquarabia waterpark in Riyadh and a FlowRider installation at Six Flags Qiddiya City. The projects demonstrate the increasing scale and technical ambition of water-based leisure investments, particularly in the Middle East. For FEC developers planning aquatic components, WhiteWater's project portfolio offers useful benchmarks for what is achievable at different investment levels.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline and Adventure Park Opens at Rivergate, Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has launched a high-energy family entertainment venue at Rivergate in Cape Town, South Africa, offering trampoline and adventure-based attractions. The opening is part of a broader global expansion of the trampoline park and indoor adventure format into emerging markets. FEC operators and franchisors can look to this opening as evidence of continued international appetite for active family entertainment concepts.

articles.title: Rage Room Attraction Set to Open in Kitimat, British Columbia
articles.summary: A new rage room concept called Breaking Point is preparing to open in Kitimat, BC, adding to the growing segment of alternative experiential entertainment within the FEC industry. Rage rooms, where guests pay to smash objects in a controlled environment, have found a niche audience seeking stress relief and novel social experiences. FEC operators exploring low-capex experiential add-ons may find the rage room format worth evaluating for their own mix.

articles.title: ISmash Breakout Experience Coming to Rapid City in May
articles.summary: ISmash, a smash-room and breakout experience brand, is expanding to Rapid City, South Dakota, continuing the national rollout of destruction-themed entertainment venues. The concept targets adults and groups seeking cathartic, hands-on experiences distinct from traditional FEC offerings. Operators looking to differentiate their venues or add adult-oriented revenue streams should monitor the growth trajectory of this format.

articles.title: Disneyland Moves Toward Cashless Operations
articles.summary: Disneyland is preparing for a cashless future, signaling a major shift in how one of the world's most-visited theme parks will handle guest transactions. Cashless systems reduce cash handling costs, speed up transactions, and generate richer guest spending data — benefits that are equally relevant for FEC operators at any scale. This move by Disney is likely to accelerate broader industry adoption of cashless payment infrastructure across FECs and amusement parks.

articles.title: Bengaluru FEC Amoeba Relaunches as Next-Gen Sports Bar Entertainment Venue
articles.summary: Amoeba, a well-known amusement and entertainment center in Bengaluru, India, has relaunched as a next-generation sports bar, blending gaming and entertainment with a bar-and-dining concept. The relaunch reflects a broader pivot by traditional FEC operators toward adult-oriented hybrid entertainment models that drive higher per-head spending. As the India entertainment market accelerates, this format evolution offers a useful case study for operators considering an adult-centric repositioning.

articles.title: India Emerging as Next Major Market for Entertainment and Attractions
articles.summary: Industry experts are identifying India as the next transformative growth market for entertainment and recreation, driven by a middle class of 500 million people projected to grow by 310 million over the next decade. With 66 percent of India's population under age 35, demand for quality family leisure and FEC-style experiences is accelerating rapidly. FEC developers, investors, and suppliers should be actively evaluating India as a high-priority expansion target.

articles.title: A Look at the World's Leading Immersive Entertainment Experiences in 2026
articles.summary: A comprehensive review of top global immersive experiences in 2026 highlights venues spanning immersive art, themed dining, technology-driven attractions, and wellness destinations, from Meow Wolf to the Sphere in Las Vegas. For FEC operators, the immersive experience category represents both a competitive threat and a design inspiration for differentiating their own venues. Understanding what is working at the high end of immersive entertainment can help FEC operators identify scalable concepts for their own markets.

articles.title: Three Strategies to Elevate Dark Ride Experiences Using Real-Time Technology
articles.summary: Industry experts outline three approaches to improving dark ride quality, with real-time content rendering emerging as a key differentiator over traditional pre-rendered video systems, as demonstrated by major new installations like Universal Epic Universe. While large-scale dark rides are primarily a theme park format, the underlying technology principles around interactivity, visual fidelity, and personalization are increasingly applicable to FEC attractions and VR experiences. FEC operators investing in immersive attractions should familiarize themselves with where ride media technology is heading.

articles.title: How FEC and Attraction Operators Are Using Storytelling to Drive Guest Engagement
articles.summary: A panel at Blooloop's 2026 Festival of Innovation featuring executives from Kennedy Space Center, Merlin Entertainments, and EPIC Entertainment Group explored how immersive storytelling can create stronger guest connections and memorable experiences. The session emphasized giving guests a sense of agency and identity within an attraction's narrative — a technique increasingly being applied across FEC-adjacent venues. FEC operators looking to deepen guest loyalty and justify premium pricing should consider how narrative design can be integrated into their own offerings.

articles.title: Ellis & Associates Recognizes 2026 Aquatic Safety Honorees
articles.summary: Ellis & Associates, the leading aquatic safety and risk management organization, has announced its 2026 Associate and Senior Associate honorees from its pool of over 100 professional lifeguard instructor trainers. For FEC operators running water attractions, pools, or splash pads, maintaining certified aquatic safety staff is both a regulatory requirement and a liability management priority. Recognition programs like this help reinforce professional standards across the aquatic safety workforce.

articles.title: Renewable Lubricants Marks 35 Years of Eco-Friendly Products for Amusement and Other Industries
articles.summary: Renewable Lubricants, an Ohio-based manufacturer of bio-based and food-grade lubricants, celebrated its 35th anniversary, highlighting its products' relevance across amusement rides and other mechanical equipment. As sustainability becomes a growing priority for FEC operators and their guests, bio-based lubricants offer a practical way to reduce environmental impact in day-to-day ride maintenance. Operators seeking greener maintenance supply chains should be aware of the available alternatives to conventional petroleum-based products.

articles.title: Amusement Center Agrees to $300K Settlement Following Child Fatality Investigation
articles.summary: An amusement center has agreed to pay $300,000 following a government investigation into the death of a young girl at the facility. The settlement is a stark reminder of the legal and financial consequences that can follow safety failures at FEC and amusement venues. Operators should use this case to reinforce safety audits, incident response protocols, and adequate liability insurance coverage.

articles.title: Bókun Launchpad Competition Offers $10K Prize for Innovative Experience Operators in Europe
articles.summary: Bókun, a Tripadvisor-owned booking and channel management platform, has launched a competition inviting European experience operators to pitch innovative concepts for a chance to win a $10,000 cash prize plus 12 months of free booking tools and distribution on Viator and GetYourGuide. For FEC and attraction operators in Europe looking to grow their online booking presence and OTA distribution, this competition offers both funding and valuable market exposure. The prize package also includes conference access and a professionally produced video case study.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia's Tourism Economy Surges, Creating FEC and Attractions Opportunities
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's travel and tourism GDP rose 7.4 percent in 2026 to $178 billion, representing 46 percent of the entire Middle East tourism economy and growing at nearly twice the global average rate. The kingdom's domestic visitor numbers grew 16 percent year-on-year in Q1 2026, signaling strong internal demand for leisure and entertainment experiences. For FEC operators, suppliers, and investors, Saudi Arabia and the broader Middle East represent one of the most active development markets in the world right now.

articles.title: Illinois Indoor Amusement Mega-Center Highlighted as Must-Visit Family Destination
articles.summary: A large-scale indoor amusement center in Illinois is receiving consumer media attention as a standout family entertainment destination, the kind of organic coverage that drives significant foot traffic for FEC operators. Media features in consumer outlets remain a cost-effective marketing channel for FECs seeking to attract local and regional visitors. Operators should actively cultivate relationships with local and regional lifestyle media to generate similar earned coverage.

articles.title: Disneyland Tests Electric Ride Vehicle Prototype as Part of Net-Zero Push
articles.summary: Disneyland has begun testing a fully electric prototype vehicle for its Autopia ride, with a commitment to retire all gas-powered vehicles from the attraction by early 2027 as part of Disney's net-zero emissions goal for 2030. The project signals a coming wave of electrification across theme park and FEC ride fleets as environmental regulations tighten, particularly in states like California. FEC operators with go-kart or ride vehicle fleets should begin evaluating the transition timeline and cost implications of shifting to electric alternatives.
